nodes.label: Graphify Usage Rules (.claude/CLAUDE.md)
nodes.rationale: Instructs Claude to trigger the graphify skill before doing anything else when the user types /graphify, so codebase questions get answered from the persistent knowledge graph instead of raw source browsing.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge, always show token cost, never hide cohesion scores, warn before HTML viz on >5000 nodes - keeps the audit trail honest (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS) rather than presenting a polished but unverifiable graph.

nodes.label: Fast Path: Existing Graph Query
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json already exists and the user asks a natural-language question, skip Steps 1-5 and query directly - avoids re-running the expensive extraction pipeline for a question the existing graph can already answer.

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Only runs when total_words > 5000 - graph value is structural clarity, not token compression, for small corpora.

nodes.label: Node ID Format Rule
nodes.rationale: IDs must be derived from the full repo-relative path stem plus entity name, matching what the AST extractor generates, so semantic and structural extraction of the same symbol collide into one node instead of creating orphan ghost-duplicates.

nodes.label: Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: A discrete rubric of five fixed values (0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55) was adopted instead of a continuous 0-1 range because production data showed models collapsed continuous guidance into a bimodal distribution (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+); discrete anchors are easier for models to follow consistently.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Vocabulary Expansion
nodes.rationale: graphify's matcher is case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language match, so a wording mismatch between the user's question and the graph's node-label vocabulary collapses the answer to zero hits; expanding the query against the graph's own extracted vocabulary first (never inventing tokens) fixes this without hallucinating search terms.

nodes.label: Work Memory (save-result / reflect / LESSONS.md)
nodes.rationale: Saves query outcomes (useful/dead_end/corrected) back into the graph so future sessions prefer known-good sources and skip known dead ends - a self-improving feedback loop.

nodes.label: Project graphify Rules (root CLAUDE.md)
nodes.rationale: Tells Claude to prefer graphify query/path/explain (scoped subgraph output) over GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep for codebase questions, and to run graphify update . after code changes to keep the graph current at no API cost.
